name: Daniel Lawson
title: Senior Vice President, Global Solutions
aboutBio: Senior Vice President of Global Solutions in the Verizon Business Group. I lead a team of Specialized Sellers across Security, UC&C, and CX; Complex Deal Executives; Global Technology GTM Activation Partners; Pre-Sales Solution Architects; and Engineers to drive revenue and enable growth across Global Enterprise and Public Sector through opportunity ownership, solution design, and thought leadership. Global Solutions serves as the voice of the market, the customer, and the field back into the Verizon ecosystem. With 20+ years at Verizon, I have led the Signature client segment, run the Network, Applications, and Innovation team, and supported financial, manufacturing, and healthcare industry customers. I have extensive background in enterprise networking, mobility, IoT, cloud, managed services, unified communications, contact centre, and security technologies.

recommendations.text: Daniel's ability to propose commercially compelling technology or service solutions to address a client's complex business operations and assets is second to none. Being able to articulate these investments at Executive Level sets him apart from any technical IT professional I have met in the industry.

socialPosts.text: Kickstarting the Devoncroft Partners Executive Summit today — proud to be delivering the opening remarks on scalable infrastructure, reduced latency, and next-generation connectivity for the media and entertainment industry. Verizon Business is committed to enabling the future of broadcast and content delivery.

Telecoms were the only companies to rally yesterday on the S&P 500 communication services sector. Verizon, AT&T, and T-Mobile. That says something important about the underlying resilience of connectivity infrastructure in an uncertain macro environment. Networks are essential — and the market is starting to recognise that again.

NAB 2025 — and there is already a crowd at the Verizon Business booth. Our team of engineers and technologists are demonstrating what next-generation broadcast connectivity looks like in practice. Not slides. Live demos. This is how you show a customer what is possible.

visionQuotes: Global Solutions is the voice of the market, the customer, and the field — feeding intelligence back into the Verizon ecosystem.
The best technology sales is about delivering outcomes that are critical to our customers' organisational objectives.
You do not build 20 years of enterprise relationships by selling products. You build them by solving problems.
strategies: Organise Global Solutions as a portfolio of specialised capabilities — Security, CX, UC&C — rather than a generalist sales motion
Position Pre-Sales Architects as trusted advisors who design outcomes, not just configure products
Use complex deal structures to win large enterprise and public sector accounts that commodity approaches cannot address
Build thought leadership through industry events and forums to establish Verizon Business as a strategic partner, not a vendor
Leverage 20+ years of enterprise relationship depth to create trusted access at the CIO and CISO level
challenges: Managing a large, multi-capability sales organisation across Security, CX, UC&C, and complex deals simultaneously
Competing with pure-play specialists in Cybersecurity and CX who have more focused product narratives
Converting enterprise and public sector customers from point-product purchasing to strategic platform relationships
Maintaining Verizon Business revenue growth against aggressive competition from AT&T, T-Mobile, and cloud-native competitors
Aligning a diverse global team across different time zones, cultures, and customer segments
opportunities: Expand Cybersecurity managed services into enterprise accounts currently using point products
Grow Contact Centre as a Service (CCaaS) offering as enterprises move away from on-premise contact centre infrastructure
Win large public sector contracts leveraging Verizon's network reliability and security credentials
Build AI-powered solutions practices that address emerging enterprise demand for AI integration services
Deepen media and broadcast sector relationships following Devoncroft partnership and NAB presence
trendsInsights: Enterprise cybersecurity spend is shifting from perimeter defence to zero-trust and managed detection and response
Contact centre technology is undergoing rapid disruption from AI-driven automation and cloud migration
Public sector technology modernisation is accelerating following COVID-era digital service failures
Complex enterprise deals increasingly require integrated solution architectures spanning network, security, and application layers
Media and broadcast industry is investing heavily in IP-based infrastructure replacing legacy broadcast technology
competitorsPartners: AT&T Business, T-Mobile for Business (primary telecom competitors)
Palo Alto Networks, CrowdStrike (cybersecurity ecosystem partners)
Genesys, NICE, Five9 (CX and contact centre partners)
productsTechnologies: Connected Experiences / Contact Centre as a Service
targetMarkets: Global enterprise accounts across financial services, manufacturing, and healthcare
US federal and state government agencies
Retail and consumer businesses requiring CX transformation
Healthcare systems requiring secure communications and networking
kpiMetrics: Verizon Business Group annual revenue approximately $30 billion
Global Solutions team spanning Security, CX, UC&C and complex deal practices
20+ years of Verizon enterprise sales and solutions leadership
Active supporter of American Heart Association and Alzheimer's research in Dallas
Regular speaker at industry events including NAB Show and Devoncroft Executive Summit
decisionInsights: Outcome-focused — evaluates solution investments based on customer organisational impact, not product features
Deeply relationship-oriented — 20+ years at Verizon reflects long-term trust building over transactional selling
Comfortable with complexity — specifically leads the 'complex deal' practice suggesting appetite for sophisticated engagements
Industry voice — active conference speaker suggests openness to market dialogue and peer engagement
Financially rigorous — MBA in Finance and Accounting from SMU underpins commercial discipline in deal structures
risksThreats: Pure-play cybersecurity and CX competitors with more focused product narratives threatening Verizon's integrated approach
Economic uncertainty reducing enterprise technology spending and extending sales cycles
AT&T Business aggressively competing for the same Global Enterprise and Public Sector accounts
AI-native competitors entering the contact centre and security markets with disruptive propositions
Talent retention risk in a highly competitive enterprise sales market
quotes: Global Solutions serves as the voice of the market, the customer and the field back into the Verizon ecosystem.
Next generation connectivity is about scalable infrastructure and reduced latency — enabling outcomes that were not possible before.
bio: Daniel Lawson is Senior Vice President of Global Solutions at Verizon Business Group, leading Specialized Sellers across Security, UC&C, and CX; Complex Deal Executives; Global Technology GTM Partners; and Pre-Sales Solution Architects driving revenue across Global Enterprise and Public Sector. With over 20 years at Verizon, he has held leadership roles across Solutions Architecture, Network Applications and Innovation, and Signature client segments. He holds a BSc in Applied Mathematics from Texas A&M University and an MBA in Finance and Accounting from Southern Methodist University.

education.degree: Master of Business Administration, Finance & Accounting
education.institution: Southern Methodist University (Cox School of Business)
